chunk_map.heading: H1: Skills from the frontier, skills of your own
Your authored skill starts personal, then compounds
Three trust artifacts the next engineer checks
The tool flags; you make the call

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Referent resolution under a sequential deck read.

rules_evaluated.evidence: chunk 1 "What a skill is": line 6: "The second-pass walk-down against your Module 2 plan file, where Claude asked you one question at a time about unresolved branches, was patterned on Matt Pocock's `grill-me` skill" — names the source beat by module NUMBER ("Module 2"), a different file/module entirely, referenced positionally rather than by content. This lecture's host module is M3 (Earn the trust — per Lecture meta and its inclusion in earn-the-trust.md); "which module is Module 2" depends on training sequencing, exactly the position-dependency this rule bans. Notably, the maintainer audit note at line 43 ("§3 fix") asserts this exact phrase was already de-sequenced to content phrasing ("the second-pass walk-down against your plan … arrived unpackaged") — but the live body text still reads "your Module 2 plan file" verbatim, so the claimed fix was never applied to the body; the audit note is stale/inaccurate against current content.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Name the M2 beat by its content (the plan-file pressure-test walk-down, unresolved branches) without the module number, matching what the maintainer note already claims was done.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every backward reference points at a beat that exists.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The Module 2 plan-file/grill-me beat (chunk 1, line 6) does resolve against a real prior beat — plan-mode-done-right.md (Module 2) carries a plan.md pressure-test with unresolved-branch questioning. Beat exists; the flaw is positional naming (rule 2), not a dead reference.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Live-demo beats survive a text read.

rules_evaluated.evidence: chunk 6 "The tool flags; you make the call": line 37: "Skills live in specific places. The curated ones were installed as personal skills at prework... Don't invent new homes." — this bullet is about install-location logistics, not about "the tool flags, you make the call"; the header undersells/misdescribes this bullet at slide-projection distance.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Either fold the locations bullet under a header that also covers it, or split it to its own slide with a header naming what it's actually about.

rules_evaluated.evidence: No "Not X. Not Y. Z." construction (line 30's "Not '...' but:" is a single-negation contrast, not the banned triple); chunk 4's two "Three ... skills, and ..." bullets are a two-part contrastive flip, not an anaphora/epistrophe triple; no self-grading superlatives (the "most-cited piece of computer security writing" at line 11 is a factual claim about an external paper, not a value-prop leak about our own content); the Intercom 31%-of-R&D figure is hedged once with attribution, not stated-then-retracted; no abstraction-noun stacks; no empty-leg verb triplets.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Internal dialect never lands on a slide.
rules_evaluated.evidence: "surfaces" (chunk 6, "flags surfaces") is ordinary security-domain vocabulary, not repo/maintainer dialect; no other compendium/beat/mood/forcing-function/firing terms appear in the body.
